name: Stuffed Butternut Squash- Pepin
recipeIngredient: or canola oil up to 3 tablespoons
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Cut the butternut squash in half lengthwise and remove the seeds. Score the flesh of the squash by making 1/2-inch deep cuts through it, one way and then the other (checkerboard). Place the squash halves cut side up on a cookie sheet and bake for about 60 minutes until tender when pierced with a fork.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons of canola oil in a large skillet. When hot, add scallions and sauté for 1-1/2 minutes. Mix in garlic and ginger, then set the pan aside off the heat.
4. Once the squash is cool enough to handle, gently scoop the flesh from the shells using a spoon, reserving the shells. Add the pulp, along with salt and pepper, to the scallions mixture. Stir until combined but still chunky.
5. Fill the reserved shells with the mixture. In a small bowl, mix the remaining bread crumbs with 1 tablespoon of oil. Sprinkle this over the stuffed squash.
6. Place the squash halves on a cookie sheet and bake for 20 minutes at 400°F until heated through. If the crumb topping is not browned, place under a hot broiler for a few minutes.
7. Cut each squash half in half again (lengthwise) and serve one piece per person. The skin is edible. Serves 4.
INFO: Recipe from Jacques Pepin's Kitchen: Encore with Claudine, by Jacques Pepin. 1998 KQED Books, companion to the Public Television series. If you have comments or questions about this recipe, please E-mail Pat Hanneman at kitpath@earthlink.net (12/98).
